title: Senior Chemist — Pharmaceutical Synthesis (BPA / Boron Chemistry)
about: ASPL Fusion (Agnira Sanlayan Pvt. Ltd.) is an applied plasma and neutron-systems company working on accelerator-based BNCT, domestic medical-isotope production, and industrial neutron applications, within established Indian regulatory and safety frameworks. The boron pharmaceutical pathway is a critical component of any BNCT clinical programme. BPA (boronophenylalanine) is currently imported into India; developing an indigenous, GMP-grade synthesis pathway is a domain-defining engineering research project.
description: Lead ASPL's indigenous BPA synthesis programme. Develop a synthesis pathway, scale from laboratory bench through to a GMP pilot, and establish the analytical, regulatory, and documentation infrastructure required for clinical-grade production.
responsibilities: Design and optimise a BPA synthesis pathway
Develop the BPA–fructose conjugation protocol for clinical use
Scale from laboratory synthesis to a GMP pilot
Establish analytical methods for BPA purity and boron-content verification
Prepare CDSCO drug master file documentation
Coordinate with GMP QA for facility certification
requirements: PhD in Organic Chemistry, Medicinal Chemistry, or Pharmaceutical Chemistry
Experience with amino-acid synthesis, boronic-acid chemistry, or boron-containing compounds
Analytical chemistry proficiency (HPLC, NMR, MS for purity analysis)
Understanding of Indian drug regulatory pathways (CDSCO preferred)
3+ years of relevant research or industrial experience
preferred: Patent or publication record in pharmaceutical synthesis
benefits: Co-authorship on ASPL publications and patent filings (the default for technical roles)
Conference travel support (ANS, IEEE NPSS, IPAC, ASTRO, IAEA technical meetings and equivalent)
Direct mentorship from Prof. Prabhat Ranjan and Prof. Y.C. Saxena, plus weekly working-group technical syncs
Founding-team scope: shape your domain end-to-end as part of a small team scaling up
Core collaboration hours 10:00–18:00 IST with flexibility outside that window
